content: Serana: So, Dexion... ever fuck a vampire before? I mean, properly, not just as some thrall plaything for Malkus. Dexion Evicus: I—what? That's hardly an appropriate question for a Moth Priest, young lady. Serana: Oh, come on. You're surrounded by godsdamned debauchery every night now, between MJR, Lydia, and me. You can't tell me you haven't noticed. Dexion Evicus: My focus remains on the Elder Scrolls and the preservation of knowledge, not... carnal distractions. Serana: —ha! That’s the old man’s way of saying you’ve never had a mortal woman ride your cock so hard she made you forget your own name. Oh, relax. If you ever change your mind, I can arrange something... educational. Maybe tie you up, drain you just enough to make you dizzy, then let a couple of our more, ah, enthusiastic bandit captives show you what Skyrim’s really about.

Dexion Evicus: I think I’ll stick to my scrolls and my sanity, thank you. The Divines did not bless me with immortality to endure such... excesses. Serana: Suit yourself. More sinful delights for the rest of us. Though I doubt our dragonborn would share anyway—he’s got a possessive streak.

Isran: I still don't trust that purple-eyed bitch Serana lurking around here. One wrong move and I'll smash her skull in. helping Dexion Evicus: Violence won't solve everything, Isran. She provided crucial insight on the Elder Scrolls. And frankly, watching you two bicker is more exhausting than deciphering their prophecies. Isran: Fuck your diplomacy, old man. You weren't there when vampires slaughtered my entire family. Their screams, their bodies drained and left to rot—you think reading dusty scrolls compares to that? Dexion Evicus: No, I don't. But if we're sharing horrors, the last vampire who kept me captive tried to me in ways even the White-Gold Tower wouldn't sanction. Not all battles are fought with hammers. Isran: Bet you still came, though. Every Imperial I’ve met can't resist a good fuck, even with fangs involved. Dexion Evicus: You Redguards and your crude obsessions. Some of us value discipline over base instinct. Even in this goddess-forsaken orgy of a province. Isran: Yeah? Tell that to the whores of Riften. Saw one riding a troll last week—said it was the best night of her life. Skyrim’s got its perks. Dexion Evicus: And its plagues. I’ll take the comfort of my scrolls over your barbaric revelry any day.

Isran: Be glad your scrolls don't answer back, priest. Less headache that way. Now let's get back to watching for bloodsuckers before the night gets worse. Dexion Evicus: Ever the optimist. Fine. But if we must suffer this conversation, at least fetch me a bottle of Cyrodiilic brandy from your stores. Isran: Fat chance. Last time you got drunk, you tried to read cracks in the floor like they were prophecies. Get back to your damned study before I change my mind about letting you stay.

Please begin / continue a conversation topic (greetings are not needed). Ensure to change the topic if the current one is losing steam. The conversation should steer towards topics which reveal information about the characters and who they are, or instead drive forward previous conversations in their memory.

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): The scene is a dimly lit, stone-walled room with a low ceiling. The walls are constructed from large, rough-hewn stones, and the floor appears to be made of wooden planks. A small, lit lantern is placed at the edge of a barrel and illuminates the area. In the foreground, a character is visible, clad in leather armor and facing away from the viewer. They appear to be interacting with another character, who is partially obscured by text on the screen. The screen displays a conversation between the two characters, with options for the player to respond. The character, named Sarine Jurard, is speaking to the player. The text on the screen reads: "What have you got for sale? Can you tell me more about archery? Do you know anything about the old Dawnguard?" There is also an option to exit the conversation. At the bottom of the screen, there is a toolbar with various icons, including a web browser, a chat window, and several other applications. The overall atmosphere of the scene is dark and mysterious, suggesting that it may be part of a role-playing game or adventure.**

Serana: You know, Dexion, I always wondered - does your little moth cult make you take a vow of celibacy, or are you just personally bad at seduction? Because I could teach you things that'd make your precious Scrolls seem tame. Dexion Evicus: By the White-Gold Tower... must you always defile sacred matters with your crude vampiric filth?
Please wrap up the current topic between the NPCs in a natural way. Nobody is leaving, so there is no need for formal goodbyes.

Dexion Evicus: I took vows to serve knowledge and light, not to entertain your juvenile provocations. These Scrolls contain secrets that could unravel the very fabric of reality - and all you can think about is base physical pleasures? The Moth Priests exist to safeguard-

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): The scene depicts a man in dark robes standing in a dimly lit room with stone walls and a stone floor. The man is leaning over a glowing blue table, his head bent down towards the surface. He appears to be examining the glowing symbols on the table. In the background, there are two wooden doors, one of which is open, revealing a dark space beyond it. A deer head is mounted on the wall above the open door, adding to the mysterious atmosphere of the scene. Several lit candles sit on the table in front of the man, casting flickering shadows around the room. A figure stands to the right of the scene, near the closed wooden door. The figure is labeled "Dexion Evicus" and seems to be engaged in conversation with the player character, as indicated by the text "Carefully gather the bark? How?" displayed above them. This suggests that the player character is interacting with Dexion Evicus, possibly seeking guidance or information about gathering bark. Overall, the scene presents a mystical and intriguing setting, with the man's examination of the glowing table and the conversation between the player character and Dexion Evicus hinting at a deeper narrative or quest unfolding within this environment.**

Serana: Oh please, don't give me that holier-than-thou act. I bet you've got some naughty forbidden texts stashed away like every other repressed scholar. That door looks like it leads to someplace more interesting than this dusty old fort.
**MJR picked up / took Elder Scroll (Sun) from Holding Cell Safe**

Serana: Maybe a secret torture chamber? Or better yet - a skooma den. Dexion Evicus: Your fascination with depravity is matched only by your lack of respect for ancient sanctums. That door leads to the archives, not whatever debauchery your twisted mind conjures. Serana: Oh look Dexion, another glowing table full of secrets.
**Dexion Evicus stood up from a(n) Arcane Enchanter**
Serana: Tell me, do you Moth Priests ever get tired of staring at glowing shit all day? Or do you just rub one out to ancient runes when no one's looking?

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): The scene depicts a dimly lit, medieval-style room with stone walls and floors. It features various pieces of furniture, including tables, a chair and other decorative items throughout the space. A woman lies on a raised platform in the foreground, positioned on her back with her head tilted to the side. She is naked, with her arms resting at her sides. To the right, an unidentifiable dark object is visible on the ground. In the background, a fireplace crackles with flames, casting a warm glow over the area. The atmosphere suggests a cozy yet ominous setting, possibly within a castle or similar structure. The overall ambiance is one of mystery and intrigue, inviting the viewer to explore the scene further.**

Dexion Evicus: Your vulgarity knows no bounds, vampire. glowing symbols Serana: Oh look, the Dragonborn's finally getting his hands dirty with real forbidden knowledge. Dexion, you must be positively blushing under that beard - your precious Scroll being handled by a man who fucks werewolves for sport. Dexion Evicus: That is the ! You reckless fools treat divine artifacts like tavern dice! The blinding price for such ignorance comes swift and— That's it - I'm returning to the White-Gold Tower.
**Dexion Evicus unequipped Akaviri Sword** **Dexion Evicus unequipped Moth Priest Robes** **Dexion Evicus unequipped Moth Priest Sandals** **Dexion Evicus equipped nolight armor**

Dexion Evicus: The Divines clearly sent you as some form of divine test, and I'm failing spectacularly. Serana: Oh don't be such a bore. If you leave now, you'll miss watching me and MJR do more interesting things with enchanted tables. Ooooh, someone died naked and dramatic.

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): The scene depicts a woman with long, reddish-brown hair and a bare torso, leaning against a stone wall. She appears to be in a state of distress or discomfort, as indicated by her facial expression and body language. **Key Elements:** * **Woman:** The central figure is a woman with distinctive features such as long, reddish-brown hair and an exposed upper body. Her posture suggests she is leaning against a stone wall, which could imply a sense of vulnerability or distress. * **Facial Expression:** The woman's face is turned away from the viewer, but her profile reveals a look of pain or discomfort. This expression adds to the overall impression that she is experiencing some form of hardship or suffering. * **Body Language:** Her body language further supports the notion that she is not in a comfortable or relaxed state. The way she leans against the wall and the positioning of her hands on the ground convey a sense of strain or effort. * **Background:** The background of the scene is dark and moody, featuring stone walls and a metal grate or door. This setting contributes to the somber atmosphere and may suggest that the scene is set in a dungeon, prison, or other confined environment.**

Serana: Classic Skyrim foreplay. Dexion Evicus: Ancestor's ashes, you wretched creature! Someone clearly met a dark fate here, and you- Serana: -I'm just pointing out the aesthetic. Naked corpse, crackling fire... Well fuck me sideways, Dexion's finally showing some skin!

**Dexion Evicus equipped Moth Priest Robes** **Dexion Evicus equipped Moth Priest Sandals** **Dexion Evicus equipped Akaviri Sword** **Dexion Evicus unequipped Akaviri Sword** **Dexion Evicus equipped Akaviri Sword**

Serana: Been waiting centuries to see if Moth Priests still have their... ancient relics intact. Dexion Evicus: This is purely for research purposes, to better commune with— Serana: Oh spare me the scholarly bullshit. That sad little cheese block is getting more action than you've seen in decades. Well well, looks like someone's been having fun without us. Dexion, you sure you don't want to stay and watch? The show's just getting started. Dexion Evicus: I'd rather gouge my own eyes out with a rusted dagger than witness another moment of your profane existence.

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): The scene depicts a dimly lit chamber with stone walls and floor. The room is cluttered with various objects, including wooden crates, a pile of blankets or pillows, and what appears to be a large pile of firewood in the background. A fireplace is visible in the distance, emitting a warm glow that illuminates the surrounding area. A wooden table and bench are visible on the left side of the scene. In the foreground, there are several items that catch the attention. On the right side of the scene, there is person lying on the ground, covered by a blanket or cloth. The person's face is partially visible, but their is obscured by the blanket. Near the person, there is a large pile of blankets or pillows stacked haphazardly on the ground. To the left of the person, there are two wooden crates positioned side by side. The crates appear to be made of dark wood and have a rustic appearance. In front of the crates, there is a large, dark-colored rug or mat that covers most of the floor. Overall, the scene presents a cozy and intimate atmosphere, with the warm glow of the fireplace and the soft lighting creating a sense of comfort and relaxation.**
Serana: Oh look, the prudish priest is getting dressed again.
**Serana rested on / used a(n) Bench**

Serana: What's wrong Dexion? Scared MJR might enjoy the view too much? Dexion Evicus: I'd rather face a thousand vampire hordes than endure another moment of your profane commentary. This is why I preferred being locked in a vampire cave - at least those brutes had the decency to just drink blood, not subject me to your endless debauchery.
**Serana stood up from a(n) Bench**

Serana: Mmm, someone's been busy setting up quite the romantic little dungeon bunker. Cozy fire, soft blankets... Dexion, you you don't want to put those Moth Priest fingers to more creative use? Dexion Evicus: I'd sooner march naked through the Soul Cairn than lower myself to your hedonistic—

The ceiling is made of brick and features a chandelier hanging from it. A large stone fireplace is visible on the left side of the room, accompanied by a mounted animal head above it. In the center of the room, there is a woman dressed in leather armor, facing away from the viewer looking towards an open doorway. She appears to be holding a weapon. To the right of the woman, there are several wooden picnic tables arranged in the room. The atmosphere suggests a rustic or rural setting, possibly a tavern or inn. The overall ambiance is cozy and inviting, with soft lighting and warm colors creating a sense of comfort and relaxation. The details visible in the scene include:**

Serana: You call that praying? I've seen more passion in a draugr's love letters. Come on, Dexion, let's spice up this depressing little scene—I know at least five Daedric rituals more exciting than whatever sad little prayer you're mumbling. Dexion Evicus: Your mockery of faith is as tiresome as your insatiable appetite for perversion. Some of us still hold to discipline, unlike you, who treats divinity as if it were another of your cheap brothel tricks. Serana: Oh honey, if you think my tricks are Well well, looks like someone's getting a hands-on demonology lesson.

Serana: Dexion, you might want to take notes - this is how real scholars do their research.

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): The scene depicts a stone courtyard with a large, dark stain on the ground and a hand holding a stick or rod, possibly a sword. The stain appears to be blood, suggesting that there may have been a violent incident in the area. **Key Elements:** * A hand grasping a stick or rod * A large, dark stain resembling blood on the ground * Stone flooring and walls * An arched doorway in the background **Possible Interpretation:** The presence of the stain and the hand holding a weapon implies that a character may have been involved in a fight or altercation. The setting suggests a medieval or fantasy environment. **Relevant Details for NPC Discussion:** * The nature of the stain (blood or something else) * The identity of the person whose hand is visible * The context surrounding the event that led to the stain These details could be discussed by NPCs to understand what happened in the scene and how it relates to their own stories or quests.**
Dexion Evicus: By the Eight Divines...
